word_list.word: beauty is in the [eye] of the (beholder|gazer)

senses.definition: used to suggest that what one person finds beautiful or attractive may not be the same for another person, and that opinions on beauty can vary widely

examples.x: Some people may find tattoos beautiful and appealing, while others may find them unattractive, illustrating that beauty is in the eye of the beholder.

word_list.pronunciation: bjňąuňź…ĺi …™z ňąo änli …Ēňź…Ļ bňĆ Ćt skňą…™n dňąiňźp

senses.definition: used to imply that true beauty goes beyond superficial appearances and should be judged based on deeper qualities such as kindness, compassion, intelligence, and integrity

word_list.word: a good horse cannot be of a bad color
word_list.pronunciation: …ź …°ňą äd hňą…Ēňź…Ļs k√¶nňą…Ďňźt biňź …ôv…ô bňą√¶d kňą Ćl…ö

senses.definition: used to imply that the quality or worth of something is not determined by its superficial or outward appearance

word_list.word: the fairest rose is at last withered

senses.definition: used to suggest that nothing in this world is permanent, and that even the most beautiful things will eventually lose their charm and vitality over time

examples.x: No matter how beautiful or perfect something may seem, the fairest rose is at last withered, and everything must come to an end.

word_list.word: a black plum is (just|) as sweet as a white (one|plum)

senses.definition: used to imply that one should not judge the worth or value of things or people based solely on their outward appearance

examples.x: I try to appreciate the diversity of cultures and not let stereotypes influence my opinions, because a black plum is as sweet as a white one.

word_list.word: a blind man's wife needs no paint
word_list.pronunciation: …ź blňąa…™nd mňą√¶nz wňąa…™f nňąiňźdz nňąo ä pňąe…™nt

senses.definition: used to emphasize the idea that true beauty and value come from within, and that external appearances are not the most significant factor in a meaningful and fulfilling relationship

examples.x: I always remind myself that true beauty comes from within, because a blind man's wife needs no paint.

word_list.word: beauty provoketh thieves sooner than gold
word_list.pronunciation: bjňąuňź…ĺi p…Ļ…ôvňąo äk…ôőł őłňąiňźvz sňąuňźn…ö √į…źn …°ňąo äld

senses.definition: used to suggest that beautiful things are more likely to draw envy and attention, and hence more prone to being targeted by thieves than valuable material possessions like gold

examples.x: I try not to flaunt my wealth or possessions, because beauty provoketh thieves sooner than gold.

senses.definition: used to suggests that those who are well-dressed or well-groomed will be perceived as being of high quality or of greater value than those who are not

examples.x: In the business world, it's important to dress for success, as the old saying goes, fine feathers make fine birds.

word_list.pronunciation: …°…Ļňąe…™s w…™l lňą√¶st bjňąuňź…ĺi fňąe…™v…ö w…™l blňą√¶st

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is more important to develop inner qualities and virtues that will last, rather than relying solely on external beauty which is temporary

word_list.word: please your eye and plague your heart
word_list.pronunciation: plňąiňźz j ä…Ļ ňąa…™ √¶nd plňąe…™…° j ä…Ļ hňą…Ďňź…Ļt

senses.definition: used to warn that pursuing things that seem desirable or attractive on the surface, such as relationships or material possessions, may ultimately lead to disappointment or pain

word_list.word: there is many a good cock come out of a tattered bag
word_list.pronunciation: √į…õ…Ļ …™z mňą…õni …ź …°ňą äd kňą…Ďňźk kňą Ćm ňĆa ä…ĺ…ôv …ź tňą√¶…ĺ…öd bňą√¶…°

senses.definition: used to imply that talented people may come from unexpected places or backgrounds, so it is important to recognize and appreciate their worth beyond external factors

examples.x: Although he came from humble beginnings and had no formal education, his intelligence and hard work proved that there is many a good cock come out of a tattered bag.

senses.definition: used to suggest that the way a person dresses can have a significant impact on how they are perceived by others, and can even influence their own self-image

examples.x: When he was invited to a formal event, he knew that clothes make the man, and he made sure to wear a well-tailored suit and polished shoes.

word_list.word: the cowl does not make the monk
word_list.pronunciation: √į…ô kňąa äl d ĆznňĆ…Ďňźt mňĆe…™k √į…ô mňą Ćnk

senses.definition: used to say that a person's clothing or external appearance does not necessarily reflect their true character or identity

examples.x: Just because he dresses in a suit and tie doesn't mean he's trustworthy, the cowl does not make the monk.

word_list.word: beauty draws with a single hair

senses.definition: used to say that the power of beauty, that goes beyond mere physical ability, is irresistibly alluring and immeasurable
used to say that physical beauty is short-lived, just like a flower that withers and fades away quickly after blooming
used to say that physical beauty is not an inherited trait and is therefore not a reliable measure of a person's worth or value

examples.x: Although her mother was known for her striking beauty, she never took it for granted and always reminded her daughter that beauty is no inheritance.

senses.definition: used to convey that true beauty is not just about one's physical appearance but is also reflected in one's actions, behavior, and character
